addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: If the Chrome browser tools are deferred (must be loaded via ToolSearch before use), load them with ToolSearch before calling them, and batch every tool you expect to need into ONE ToolSearch call (the select query accepts a comma-separated list). Do NOT load tools one at a time; each separate ToolSearch call wastes a full round-trip.**

Start a browser task whose tools are not yet loaded with a single call loading the core set:

ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp,mcp__claude-in-chrome__navigate,mcp__claude-in-chrome__computer,mcp__claude-in-chrome__read_page,mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_create_mcp"

Add task-specific tools to the same call when the task obviously needs them: read_console_messages / read_network_requests for debugging, form_input for forms, gif_creator for recordings, javascript_tool for page scripting. Only issue a second ToolSearch if the task later needs a tool you did not anticipate.

## plugin:github:github
The GitHub MCP Server provides tools to interact with GitHub platform.

Tool selection guidance:
	1. Use 'list_*' tools for broad, simple retrieval and pagination of all items of a type (e.g., all issues, all PRs, all branches) with basic filtering.
	2. Use 'search_*' tools for targeted queries with specific criteria, keywords, or complex filters (e.g., issues with certain text, PRs by author, code containing functions).

Context management:
	1. Use pagination whenever possible with batches of 5-10 items.
	2. Use minimal_output parameter set to true if the full information is not needed to accomplish a task.

Tool usage guidance:
	1. For 'search_*' tools: Use separate 'sort' and 'order' parameters if available for sorting results - do not include 'sort:' syntax in query strings. Query strings should contain only search criteria (e.g., 'org:google language:python'), not sorting instructions. Always call 'get_me' first to understand current user permissions and context. ## Issues

Check 'list_issue_types' first for organizations to use proper issue types. Use 'search_issues' before creating new issues to avoid duplicates. Always set 'state_reason' when closing issues. ## Pull Requests

PR review workflow: Always use 'pull_request_review_write' with method 'create' to create a pending review, then 'add_comment_to_pending_review' to add comments, and finally 'pull_request_review_write' with method 'submit_pending' to submit the review for complex reviews with line-specific comments.

Before creating a pull request, search for pull request templates in the repository. Template files are called pull_request_template.md or they're located in '.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE' directory. Use the template content to structure the PR description and then call create_pull_request tool.

attachment.content:
- defuddle: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page. Do NOT use for URLs ending in .md — those are already markdown, use WebFetch directly.
- obsidian-bases: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.
- obsidian-cli: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM. Use when the user asks to interact with their Obsidian vault, manage notes, search vault content, perform vault operations from the command line, or develop and debug Obsidian plugins and themes.
- obsidian-markdown: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.
- kickoff: One-time household setup — interview the family and write their real config files
- plan-week: Draft the week's dinners from the family's calendar + preferences, then (on approval) write the grocery list, prep reminders, and per-dinner Google Docs
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
# Build Tasks — Family Meal-Planning Workflow

Progress tracker so we can step away and pick back up cleanly. Spec lives in
[`requirements.md`](requirements.md). Diary of what happened is in
[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md).

**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

**Last updated:** 2026-07-10

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, 1 committed (`963a8b1`); Google Docs setup + doc updates committed
  (`d3cdc4e`) and `/kickoff` committed (`03a89d1`) — all three **pushed to `origin/main`**.
- **Google Docs verified end-to-end** this session: `listDocuments` (read/auth OK) +
  `createDocument` (write OK, content rendered) + `deleteFile` (cleanup) — the OAuth gating
  step is DONE.
- Phase 3 mechanism built: **`/plan-week`** slash-command (`.claude/commands/plan-week.md`,
  untracked) — full flow (calendar → select → grocery list → Google Docs + reminders).
- **`/kickoff` run for real** — `config/*.md` written for the Jones family, plus 4 seed
  recipes imported from URLs (via Defuddle CLI, now noted as a README dependency).
- **NEXT:** commit `/plan-week`; then run `/plan-week` for real against the new config to
  exercise the full flow. **Still parked:** runtime/scheduling home (Phase 5).

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
=====PROGRESS=====
# Progress Notes — Family Cookbook Workflow

A running diary of steps taken while building out this workflow.

## 2026-07-09
- Added `/Users/****/Documents/mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` as a working directory (project-scoped, saved to `07_2026_family-cookbook/.claude/settings.local.json`).
- Created blank `requirements.md` in the mp-docs folder.
- Created this progress notes file to passively record build steps.
- Researched Claude Code options for Apple Reminders integration. Two mechanisms: MCP servers vs. skills; two bridges: AppleScript (slow) vs. EventKit (fast). Top candidates: `rem` (EventKit skill, fastest, Reminders-only), FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar), automating-mac-apps plugin (broad Apple-app automation). All run locally on macOS and need Reminders permission.
- Inspected the automating-mac-apps plugin: 16 skills (Reminders + Calendar/Mail/Notes/office suite/etc.), AppleScript-based, community repo. Reminders skill is self-contained. Concluded it's overkill for a Reminders-focused workflow.
- **Decision:** build on FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar). Verified: Node v25.3.0 (needs 20+), package v1.5.0 ships pre-built signed binary (no Xcode via npx). Tools: reminders_tasks, reminders_subtasks, reminders_lists, calendar_events, calendar_calendars.
- Installed it project-scoped via `07_2026_family-cookbook/.mcp.json` (server name `apple-events`). Pending: restart/approve the MCP server in Claude Code, then grant macOS Reminders/Calendar permission (prompts as "event") on first call.
- Interviewed on requirements (2 rounds) and wrote `requirements.md` from the Project Plan. Key decisions: hybrid recipe sourcing (personal MD collection via link/photo extraction + AI/web gap-fill); grocery list grouped by ranked stores (full price optimization deferred to out-of-scope); configurable dinner count with the weekly run starting by reading Apple Calendar for dinners-out; config stored as project markdown files + shareable `.example` templates (real files gitignored), grocery list output to Apple Notes; **Apple Calendar** via apple-events instead of Google Calendar; end-of-week ratings prompt as part of a scheduled routine; recipes handed to mobile via Apple Notes; per-recipe prep reminders in a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list; portion/scaling strategy captured as a per-family kickoff config; weekly run = scheduled routine + manual trigger.
- Moved deferred items (full price optimization, breakfast/lunch, auto-ordering, nutrition tracking) into the Project Plan's out-of-scope section.
- Open follow-ups logged in requirements.md: Google→Apple Calendar migration, ratings file schema, scheduled-routine mechanics.
- Finalized the ratings loop: 1–5 scale **+ free-text notes** (notes carry the signal), one rating per recipe stored in that recipe's own frontmatter (dropped the standalone ratings file), used to boost/suppress and to surface *similar* highly-rated recipes from note patterns.
- Split scheduled automation into two independent jobs (new §5.8): recipe-reviewer (twice weekly, Wed+Fri PM) and meal-planner (kickoff-configured day/time, pre-drafts then waits for approval); both notify via push notification. Kickoff §5.1 gained a meal-planner run-timing config item.
- Vault `mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` became inaccessible (EPERM on read/ls even with the Claude Code sandbox disabled → macOS TCC / File-Provider access loss, not the sandbox). Fix = grant Full Disk Access to the terminal/Claude Code app.
- Worked around the lock by copying the docs (Project Plan, requirements, progress-notes) into the project at `07_2026_family-cookbook/docs/`. Promoted the updated requirements to `requirements.md`; archived the prior version as `requirements.old.md`. Project docs now live in-repo rather than the vault.
- Git hygiene: staged `docs/requirements.md` (the shareable spec) + `.gitignore`; gitignored the personal/working docs (`Project Plan.md`, `progress-notes.md`, `requirements.old.md`).
- **Verified the apple-events MCP end-to-end:** Calendar read (23 calendars, weekly events) and Reminders create+delete all work; no permission errors (macOS access already granted). Learned: reminder titles reject emoji/em-dashes (plain text only); the MCP has **no Apple Notes tool** (Calendar + Reminders only), so grocery-list-to-Notes needs a separate mechanism (osascript / plugin / alt MCP) — flagged as a risk. Also confirmed calendar reads pull all calendars (sports/holiday noise) → will use a per-family "calendars to watch" allowlist; Calendar stays read-only, writes go only to a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list.
- Created `docs/build-tasks.md` as a phased build tracker (gitignored working doc) so work can pause/resume cleanly.
- **Phase 1 (Foundation) built.** Split config templates: `config/household.example.md` (address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy, default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist), plus `dietary`, `pantry-staples`, `store-rankings`, `equipment`. Recipe schema `recipes/_template.md` uses structured frontmatter ingredients (qty/unit/aisle/staple) to drive grocery aggregation, plus the locked ratings fields. Two seed recipes (sheet-pan chicken fajitas, beef taco bowls) that deliberately share peppers/onion/lime/spices to exercise shared-ingredient logic. `.gitignore` now ignores real `config/*.md` while keeping `*.example.md` templates + the recipe collection tracked — verified with `git check-ignore`.
- **Decision (ratings notes):** keep a single *current* note per recipe (`rating_notes`, overwritten on re-rate) — considered but declined an append-only `rating_history` log. Rationale: matches the locked "one rating per recipe, overwritten" spec (§5.7), keeps recipe files simple, and the current note is what drives boost/suppress + find-similar.
- **Committed Phase 1** to `main` (`963a8b1`, 10 files): config templates, recipe schema + 2 seeds, `.gitignore`, and `requirements.md`. Gitignored working docs correctly excluded.
- **Decision (outputs) + finding:** split the mobile destinations — grocery list → Apple **Reminders "Groceries"** list (checkable; better shopping UX; uses the working MCP, no new deps); recipes/menu → Apple **Notes** (phone cooking UX). The apple-events MCP can't write Notes. Discovered the user already has the official Anthropic **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** DXT installed (Claude Desktop; `add_note`/`update_note_content`) — cleaner than raw osascript, but it's a Desktop extension not wired into Claude Code (machine-specific path, awkward for sharing) and won't be present in headless scheduled runs. Open: run planner in Claude Desktop (extension auto-loads) vs. `.mcp.json` vs. osascript (portable). Requirements §5.4/5.5/5.6/6/7 updated.
- **Google Docs mechanism chosen + wired:** picked the open-source `@a-bonus/google-docs-mcp` (markdown-native — `createFolder`/`createDocument`/`replaceDocumentWithMarkdown`, exactly our per-dinner-from-markdown pipeline) over the read-only Claude.ai Drive connector. Added it to `.mcp.json` scoped to `docs,drive`, referencing `${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET}` env vars so no secret is committed. Wrote a shareable first-run setup guide `docs/google-docs-mcp-setup.md` (GCP project → Docs/Drive APIs → OAuth consent → Desktop OAuth client → env vars → `npx … auth` → restart) and added a Setup section to README. Pending: user completes GCP OAuth, restarts Claude Code, then verify by creating a test doc/folder.
- **Decision (recipes delivery):** use **Google Docs instead of Apple Notes** — one Doc per dinner, grouped in a shared weekly Drive folder (easier day-to-day navigation; share the top folder once). Reason: Google Docs is a headless API (no macOS UI-automation / Mac-awake fragility), and new-doc-per-recipe is create-only = the most reliable Docs pattern. Recipe markdown in the repo stays source of truth; each Doc is generated from it. Gating step before building: Google OAuth (Drive/Docs) + confirm the connected Google MCP can create a Doc with content (may be Drive-only → fall back to Docs API). Requirements §5.5/6/7 updated. Calendar/Reminders stay Apple + local. Menu index = the weekly folder itself (option a; no separate menu doc). Backup delivery to explore if the Claude.ai Drive connector can't create docs: the `a-bonus/google-docs-mcp` server.
- **Built `/kickoff`** as a project slash-command (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`): reads the `.example` templates, interviews in small groups (basics → members+dietary → kitchen → shopping → scheduling → optional favorite recipes), lists the user's real calendars via the apple-events MCP so they choose the watch allowlist, then writes the gitignored `config/*.md` files after confirmation. Mechanism only — actually running it (collecting real personal data) is a separate user-driven step. Currently untracked/uncommitted.
- Resolved the last two §9 open items. **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts anyway with defaults (configured dinner count + full family home) and flags the assumption. **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize the draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created); added a **shopping day** field to kickoff §5.1 to anchor that deadline. **Ratings frontmatter schema** locked: `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (structured find-similar tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`. §9 now has no open items — requirements are build-ready.

**Last updated:** 2026-07-10

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, 1 committed (`963a8b1`); Google Docs setup + doc updates committed
  (`d3cdc4e`) and `/kickoff` committed (`03a89d1`) — all three **pushed to `origin/main`**.
- **Google Docs verified end-to-end** this session: `listDocuments` (read/auth OK) +
  `createDocument` (write OK, content rendered) + `deleteFile` (cleanup) — the OAuth gating
  step is DONE.
- Phase 3 mechanism built: **`/plan-week`** slash-command (`.claude/commands/plan-week.md`,

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
=====PROGRESS=====
# Progress Notes — Family Cookbook Workflow

A running diary of steps taken while building out this workflow.

## 2026-07-09
- Added `/Users/****/Documents/mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` as a working directory (project-scoped, saved to `07_2026_family-cookbook/.claude/settings.local.json`).
- Created blank `requirements.md` in the mp-docs folder.
- Created this progress notes file to passively record build steps.
- Researched Claude Code options for Apple Reminders integration. Two mechanisms: MCP servers vs. skills; two bridges: AppleScript (slow) vs. EventKit (fast). Top candidates: `rem` (EventKit skill, fastest, Reminders-only), FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar), automating-mac-apps plugin (broad Apple-app automation). All run locally on macOS and need Reminders permission.
- Inspected the automating-mac-apps plugin: 16 skills (Reminders + Calendar/Mail/Notes/office suite/etc.), AppleScript-based, community repo. Reminders skill is self-contained. Concluded it's overkill for a Reminders-focused workflow.
- **Decision:** build on FradSer/mcp-server-apple-events (EventKit MCP, Reminders + Calendar). Verified: Node v25.3.0 (needs 20+), package v1.5.0 ships pre-built signed binary (no Xcode via npx). Tools: reminders_tasks, reminders_subtasks, reminders_lists, calendar_events, calendar_calendars.
- Installed it project-scoped via `07_2026_family-cookbook/.mcp.json` (server name `apple-events`). Pending: restart/approve the MCP server in Claude Code, then grant macOS Reminders/Calendar permission (prompts as "event") on first call.
- Interviewed on requirements (2 rounds) and wrote `requirements.md` from the Project Plan. Key decisions: hybrid recipe sourcing (personal MD collection via link/photo extraction + AI/web gap-fill); grocery list grouped by ranked stores (full price optimization deferred to out-of-scope); configurable dinner count with the weekly run starting by reading Apple Calendar for dinners-out; config stored as project markdown files + shareable `.example` templates (real files gitignored), grocery list output to Apple Notes; **Apple Calendar** via apple-events instead of Google Calendar; end-of-week ratings prompt as part of a scheduled routine; recipes handed to mobile via Apple Notes; per-recipe prep reminders in a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list; portion/scaling strategy captured as a per-family kickoff config; weekly run = scheduled routine + manual trigger.
- Moved deferred items (full price optimization, breakfast/lunch, auto-ordering, nutrition tracking) into the Project Plan's out-of-scope section.
- Open follow-ups logged in requirements.md: Google→Apple Calendar migration, ratings file schema, scheduled-routine mechanics.
- Finalized the ratings loop: 1–5 scale **+ free-text notes** (notes carry the signal), one rating per recipe stored in that recipe's own frontmatter (dropped the standalone ratings file), used to boost/suppress and to surface *similar* highly-rated recipes from note patterns.
- Split scheduled automation into two independent jobs (new §5.8): recipe-reviewer (twice weekly, Wed+Fri PM) and meal-planner (kickoff-configured day/time, pre-drafts then waits for approval); both notify via push notification. Kickoff §5.1 gained a meal-planner run-timing config item.
- Vault `mp-docs-synced/workflow-tools/family-cookbook` became inaccessible (EPERM on read/ls even with the Claude Code sandbox disabled → macOS TCC / File-Provider access loss, not the sandbox). Fix = grant Full Disk Access to the terminal/Claude Code app.
- Worked around the lock by copying the docs (Project Plan, requirements, progress-notes) into the project at `07_2026_family-cookbook/docs/`. Promoted the updated requirements to `requirements.md`; archived the prior version as `requirements.old.md`. Project docs now live in-repo rather than the vault.
- Git hygiene: staged `docs/requirements.md` (the shareable spec) + `.gitignore`; gitignored the personal/working docs (`Project Plan.md`, `progress-notes.md`, `requirements.old.md`).
- **Verified the apple-events MCP end-to-end:** Calendar read (23 calendars, weekly events) and Reminders create+delete all work; no permission errors (macOS access already granted). Learned: reminder titles reject emoji/em-dashes (plain text only); the MCP has **no Apple Notes tool** (Calendar + Reminders only), so grocery-list-to-Notes needs a separate mechanism (osascript / plugin / alt MCP) — flagged as a risk. Also confirmed calendar reads pull all calendars (sports/holiday noise) → will use a per-family "calendars to watch" allowlist; Calendar stays read-only, writes go only to a dedicated "Family Meals" Reminders list.
- Created `docs/build-tasks.md` as a phased build tracker (gitignored working doc) so work can pause/resume cleanly.
- **Phase 1 (Foundation) built.** Split config templates: `config/household.example.md` (address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy, default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist), plus `dietary`, `pantry-staples`, `store-rankings`, `equipment`. Recipe schema `recipes/_template.md` uses structured frontmatter ingredients (qty/unit/aisle/staple) to drive grocery aggregation, plus the locked ratings fields. Two seed recipes (sheet-pan chicken fajitas, beef taco bowls) that deliberately share peppers/onion/lime/spices to exercise shared-ingredient logic. `.gitignore` now ignores real `config/*.md` while keeping `*.example.md` templates + the recipe collection tracked — verified with `git check-ignore`.
- **Decision (ratings notes):** keep a single *current* note per recipe (`rating_notes`, overwritten on re-rate) — considered but declined an append-only `rating_history` log. Rationale: matches the locked "one rating per recipe, overwritten" spec (§5.7), keeps recipe files simple, and the current note is what drives boost/suppress + find-similar.
- **Committed Phase 1** to `main` (`963a8b1`, 10 files): config templates, recipe schema + 2 seeds, `.gitignore`, and `requirements.md`. Gitignored working docs correctly excluded.
- **Decision (outputs) + finding:** split the mobile destinations — grocery list → Apple **Reminders "Groceries"** list (checkable; better shopping UX; uses the working MCP, no new deps); recipes/menu → Apple **Notes** (phone cooking UX). The apple-events MCP can't write Notes. Discovered the user already has the official Anthropic **"Read and Write Apple Notes"** DXT installed (Claude Desktop; `add_note`/`update_note_content`) — cleaner than raw osascript, but it's a Desktop extension not wired into Claude Code (machine-specific path, awkward for sharing) and won't be present in headless scheduled runs. Open: run planner in Claude Desktop (extension auto-loads) vs. `.mcp.json` vs. osascript (portable). Requirements §5.4/5.5/5.6/6/7 updated.
- **Google Docs mechanism chosen + wired:** picked the open-source `@a-bonus/google-docs-mcp` (markdown-native — `createFolder`/`createDocument`/`replaceDocumentWithMarkdown`, exactly our per-dinner-from-markdown pipeline) over the read-only Claude.ai Drive connector. Added it to `.mcp.json` scoped to `docs,drive`, referencing `${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET}` env vars so no secret is committed. Wrote a shareable first-run setup guide `docs/google-docs-mcp-setup.md` (GCP project → Docs/Drive APIs → OAuth consent → Desktop OAuth client → env vars → `npx … auth` → restart) and added a Setup section to README. Pending: user completes GCP OAuth, restarts Claude Code, then verify by creating a test doc/folder.
- **Decision (recipes delivery):** use **Google Docs instead of Apple Notes** — one Doc per dinner, grouped in a shared weekly Drive folder (easier day-to-day navigation; share the top folder once). Reason: Google Docs is a headless API (no macOS UI-automation / Mac-awake fragility), and new-doc-per-recipe is create-only = the most reliable Docs pattern. Recipe markdown in the repo stays source of truth; each Doc is generated from it. Gating step before building: Google OAuth (Drive/Docs) + confirm the connected Google MCP can create a Doc with content (may be Drive-only → fall back to Docs API). Requirements §5.5/6/7 updated. Calendar/Reminders stay Apple + local. Menu index = the weekly folder itself (option a; no separate menu doc). Backup delivery to explore if the Claude.ai Drive connector can't create docs: the `a-bonus/google-docs-mcp` server.
- **Built `/kickoff`** as a project slash-command (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`): reads the `.example` templates, interviews in small groups (basics → members+dietary → kitchen → shopping → scheduling → optional favorite recipes), lists the user's real calendars via the apple-events MCP so they choose the watch allowlist, then writes the gitignored `config/*.md` files after confirmation. Mechanism only — actually running it (collecting real personal data) is a separate user-driven step. Currently untracked/uncommitted.
- Resolved the last two §9 open items. **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts anyway with defaults (configured dinner count + full family home) and flags the assumption. **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize the draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created); added a **shopping day** field to kickoff §5.1 to anchor that deadline. **Ratings frontmatter schema** locked: `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (structured find-similar tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`. §9 now has no open items — requirements are build-ready.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build Tasks — Family Meal-Planning Workflow

Progress tracker so we can step away and pick back up cleanly. Spec lives in
[`requirements.md`](requirements.md). Diary of what happened is in
[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md).

**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

**Last updated:** 2026-07-10

---

## 👉 Current status / next step

- Phases 0, 0.5, 1 committed (`963a8b1`); Google Docs setup + doc updates committed
  (`d3cdc4e`) and `/kickoff` committed (`03a89d1`) — all three **pushed to `origin/main`**.
- **Google Docs verified end-to-end** this session: `listDocuments` (read/auth OK) +
  `createDocument` (write OK, content rendered) + `deleteFile` (cleanup) — the OAuth gating
  step is DONE.
- Phase 3 mechanism built: **`/plan-week`** slash-command (`.claude/commands/plan-week.md`,
  untracked) — full flow (calendar → select → grocery list → Google Docs + reminders).
- **`/kickoff` run for real** — `config/*.md` written for the Jones family, plus 4 seed
  recipes imported from URLs (via Defuddle CLI, now noted as a README dependency).
- **NEXT:** commit `/plan-week`; then run `/plan-week` for real against the new config to
  exercise the full flow. **Still parked:** runtime/scheduling home (Phase 5).

---

## Phase 0 — Requirements & decisions  `[x]`
- [x] Requirements interview + spec written (`requirements.md`)
- [x] Consolidated the updated requirements into the repo; archived old version
- [x] Git: track `requirements.md`, gitignore personal/working docs
- [x] Decide empty-calendar behavior → draft w/ defaults, flag assumption
- [x] Decide no-approval behavior → auto-finalize eve before shopping day
- [x] Decide ratings frontmatter schema → `rating`, `rating_notes`, `liked_traits`, `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`

## Phase 0.5 — MCP verification  `[x]`
- [x] Load `apple-events` tool schemas
- [x] Calendar: list calendars (23) + read week's events (works)
- [x] Reminders: list lists (3) + create + delete (works, cleaned up)
- [x] Note constraints discovered (see Constraints section)

## Phase 1 — Foundation  `[x]`
- [x] Config file layout decided → split files (household + dietary + pantry + stores + equipment)
- [x] `config/household.example.md` — address, members, budget, prep-time, portion strategy,
      default dinner count, run day/time, shopping day, calendars-to-watch allowlist
- [x] `config/dietary.example.md` — per-person preferences + allergies + avoid
- [x] `config/pantry-staples.example.md` — on-hand items excluded from lists
- [x] `config/store-rankings.example.md` — ranked stores
- [x] `config/equipment.example.md` — available cooking equipment
- [x] `recipes/_template.md` — full frontmatter schema (structured ingredients w/ aisle+staple) + ratings fields + body
- [x] `.gitignore`: `config/*.md` ignored, `*.example.md` kept; recipes tracked (verified w/ git check-ignore)
- [x] Seed recipes: `sheet-pan-chicken-fajitas.md`, `beef-taco-bowls.md` (deliberately share ingredients)

## Phase 2 — Kickoff  `[x]`
- [x] Decide skill vs slash-command → slash-command `/kickoff` (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`)
- [x] Build kickoff flow: interview → write real config files from templates
- [x] Include calendar-allowlist selection (lists user's calendars via MCP, user picks watched)
- [x] **Run `/kickoff` for real** to write `config/*.md` (Jones family: household, dietary,
      pantry-staples, store-rankings, equipment — plus 4 seed recipes imported from URLs)

## Phase 3 — Weekly meal-planner  `[~]`  (mechanism built as `/plan-week`; run pending)
- [x] Decide mechanism → slash-command `/plan-week` (`.claude/commands/plan-week.md`)
- [x] Read watched calendars for the week → who's home / dinners-out
- [x] Determine # dinners needed (configurable; empty-calendar → default + flag)
- [x] Select recipes: hybrid sourcing, honor dietary/prep/budget/equipment (+ ratings boost/suppress)
- [x] Favor shared ingredients; assign meals to days (portion strategy applied)
- [x] Build store-grouped grocery list; exclude pantry staples + `staple: true`
- [x] Write grocery list → Apple **Reminders "Groceries" list** (checkable; via MCP)
- [x] Schedule prep reminders → Reminders **"Family Meals"** list (create if missing; per-recipe timing)
- [x] Write recipes → **Google Docs** (one Doc per dinner in a shared weekly folder; create-only)
- [x] Pre-draft → approval flow; no-approval → auto-finalize at deadline (Phase 5 scheduler)
- [x] Output menu + recipes + step-by-step
- [ ] **Run `/plan-week` for real** (needs `config/*.md` from `/kickoff`; user-driven)

## Phase 4 — Recipe reviewer & import  `[ ]`
- [ ] recipe-reviewer: prompt to rate recent meals → update frontmatter fields
- [ ] recipe-import: paste link (WebFetch/defuddle) or photo → structured recipe md

## Phase 5 — Scheduled automation  `[ ]`
- [!] Decide runtime/scheduling home (Claude Code routines/CronCreate) + push mechanism
- [ ] Schedule recipe-reviewer (Wed + Fri PM)
- [ ] Schedule meal-planner (family's preferred day/time; pre-draft-then-wait)
- [ ] Wire push notifications for both

## Phase 6 — Share polish  `[ ]`
- [ ] README: clone-and-run instructions + MCP setup + permissions
- [ ] Verify `.gitignore` hides all personal data; ship `.example` templates only
- [ ] Optional: sample/example household so a new family can dry-run

---

## Decisions locked (ref)
- **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts with defaults (config dinner count + full family home), flags the assumption in output.
- **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created).
- **Ratings schema** (recipe frontmatter): `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`.
- **Calendar = read-only.** Workflow never writes calendar events. Local Apple writes go only to Apple Reminders ("Family Meals" prep + "Groceries" shopping); recipes go to Google Docs.
- **Output split:** grocery list → Reminders "Groceries" (checkable = better shopping UX); recipes → Google Docs, one Doc per dinner in a shared weekly folder (reliable headless API); prep reminders → Reminders "Family Meals".

## Constraints & gotchas discovered
- Reminder **titles reject emoji / em-dashes** — plain text + basic punctuation only. (Affects prep-reminder formatting.)
- macOS grants Calendar/Reminders access **all-or-nothing**; per-calendar scoping is enforced by our config (read allowlist + fixed write target), not the OS.
- Calendar reads pull **all** calendars → sports/holiday noise. Use the watch allowlist.
- The `mp-docs-synced` Obsidian vault is EPERM-locked (macOS TCC); project docs now live in-repo. Fix = grant Full Disk Access.

## Open risks / questions
- **[x] Recipes → Google Docs (VERIFIED 2026-07-10):** **`@a-bonus/google-docs-mcp`** (markdown-native `createFolder`/`createDocument`), scoped `docs,drive`, wired in `.mcp.json` via `${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID/SECRET}` (no secrets committed). Setup guide `docs/google-docs-mcp-setup.md` + README Setup section. User completed GCP OAuth; verified end-to-end in-session (list + create-with-content + trash cleanup, auth'd as the user). Menu index = the weekly folder itself. `/plan-week` Step 7C uses this.
- **[!] Runtime & scheduling:** where the scheduled jobs + push notifications run (Claude Code routines vs. other). Decide before Phase 5.

+[`progress-notes.md`](progress-notes.md). Issues found during testing go in
**Legend:** `[ ]` todo · `[~]` in progress · `[x]` done · `[!]` blocked/needs decision

content.text: Now the progress-notes diary entry. Let me find the end of the file to append under the right date.

content.content:
- **Decision (recipes delivery):** use **Google Docs instead of Apple Notes** — one Doc per dinner, grouped in a shared weekly Drive folder (easier day-to-day navigation; share the top folder once). Reason: Google Docs is a headless API (no macOS UI-automation / Mac-awake fragility), and new-doc-per-recipe is create-only = the most reliable Docs pattern. Recipe markdown in the repo stays source of truth; each Doc is generated from it. Gating step before building: Google OAuth (Drive/Docs) + confirm the connected Google MCP can create a Doc with content (may be Drive-only → fall back to Docs API). Requirements §5.5/6/7 updated. Calendar/Reminders stay Apple + local. Menu index = the weekly folder itself (option a; no separate menu doc). Backup delivery to explore if the Claude.ai Drive connector can't create docs: the `a-bonus/google-docs-mcp` server.
- **Built `/kickoff`** as a project slash-command (`.claude/commands/kickoff.md`): reads the `.example` templates, interviews in small groups (basics → members+dietary → kitchen → shopping → scheduling → optional favorite recipes), lists the user's real calendars via the apple-events MCP so they choose the watch allowlist, then writes the gitignored `config/*.md` files after confirmation. Mechanism only — actually running it (collecting real personal data) is a separate user-driven step. Currently untracked/uncommitted.
- Resolved the last two §9 open items. **Empty calendar** → meal-planner drafts anyway with defaults (configured dinner count + full family home) and flags the assumption. **No approval by deadline** → auto-finalize the draft the evening before the shopping day (grocery list + reminders still created); added a **shopping day** field to kickoff §5.1 to anchor that deadline. **Ratings frontmatter schema** locked: `rating` (1–5, overwritten), `rating_notes` (free text), `liked_traits` (structured find-similar tags), `last_cooked`, `times_cooked`. §9 now has no open items — requirements are build-ready.

## 2026-07-10
- **Verified Google Docs MCP end-to-end** (user had completed the GCP OAuth setup): `listDocuments` returned real Drive docs (auth OK), `createDocument` wrote a throwaway doc with rendered markdown content (write OK), `deleteFile` trashed it (cleanup). Authenticated as the user, scoped to `docs,drive`. The Phase-3 recipe-delivery gating step is now cleared.
- **Git housekeeping:** re-split the earlier combined commit into two — `d3cdc4e` (Google Docs delivery: `.mcp.json` + setup guide + README/requirements updates) and `03a89d1` (`/kickoff` command) — then **pushed all local commits to `origin/main` in order** (Phase 1 `963a8b1` first, then the two). Remote `github.com/michael13parker/family-cookbook` is now current; nothing ahead.
- **Phase 3 (weekly meal-planner) mechanism built** as `/plan-week` (`.claude/commands/plan-week.md`). Full flow: preflight-load real `config/*.md` (bail to `/kickoff` if missing) → read watched calendars (read-only, allowlist-filtered) → dinner count (empty-cal → default + flag) → hybrid recipe select honoring hard allergies/diet/equipment + soft avoid/prep/budget/prefs + ratings boost/suppress + shared-ingredient tiebreaker → assign to days w/ portion strategy → aggregate+store-group grocery list (excl. staples & `staple:true`) → **pre-draft, show, wait for approval** → on approval write Groceries reminders (plain-text titles, no emoji/em-dash), per-recipe-timed prep reminders to "Family Meals", and one create-only Google Doc per dinner under `Family Dinners / Week of YYYY-MM-DD` (docs named `YYYY-MM-DD (Day) — Title` so the folder is the menu index), then bump `times_cooked`/`last_cooked`. Mechanism only — running it needs real config from `/kickoff`. Currently untracked.
